Quality separation remains strong across all evals: with_skill is 100% pass-rate on evals 0/1/2 versus old_skill at 75%, 33.33%, and 25%.
The strict handoff-quality scenario (eval 2) remains the most discriminating test and drives the largest quality gap.
Efficiency metrics are now populated and show higher cost for with_skill: mean time 12.5s vs 7.8s and mean tokens 645 vs 600.
This indicates a quality-vs-cost tradeoff: stronger process guidance improves correctness/structure at modest additional runtime and token usage.
